title: cakile edentula subsp. islandica
intro: Cakile edentula subsp. islandica, also known as the sea rocket or sea rocket cress, is a subspecies of the Cakile edentula plant that is native to the coastal regions of Iceland and Greenland. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of isothiocyanates and other compounds. The health benefits of consuming Cakile edentula subsp. islandica include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these benefits. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be consumed raw in salads or as a garnish, although it is essential to note that the plant may cause gastrointestinal upset in some individuals.
